museum.name: Gaming Time Machine
museum.dek: December 2018: Super Smash Bros. Ultimate turns roster completion into an event.
museum.curatorNote: Initial researched draft for December 2018. Recent enough for careful source review before verification.

sections.title: Releases
sections.subtitle: Eight notable releases, led by month-specific anchors where evidence supports them.

items.title: Super Smash Bros. Ultimate
items.body: Nintendo turns the roster itself into an archival event.
items.sourceNote: Widely listed for December 7, 2018.

items.title: Celeste
items.body: A tiny mountain becomes one of the year's clearest emotional objects.
items.sourceNote: Widely listed for January 25, 2018.

items.title: Monster Hunter: World
items.body: Capcom makes Monster Hunter feel global, social and suddenly mainstream.
items.sourceNote: 2018 release lists place the console launch on January 26.

items.title: Into the Breach
items.body: Subset Games turns tactics into a clean little museum case of consequences.
items.sourceNote: Widely listed for February 27, 2018 on PC.

items.title: Far Cry 5
items.body: Ubisoft turns rural America into a loud open-world argument.
items.sourceNote: Commonly listed for March 27, 2018.

items.title: God of War
items.body: Sony's old icon returns older, quieter and unexpectedly tender.
items.sourceNote: Widely listed for April 20, 2018.

items.title: Nintendo Labo
items.body: Cardboard becomes both toy and software, making Switch feel like a strange craft-table console.
items.sourceNote: Nintendo lists April 2018 regional launches; exact territory dates vary.

items.title: Detroit: Become Human
items.body: Quantic Dream brings branching cinematic drama into another round of argument.
items.sourceNote: Widely listed for May 25, 2018.

sections.title: Hardware
sections.subtitle: Platform, buying and industry context for the month and its wider year.

items.title: Nintendo Switch Online becomes paid
items.body: Nintendo's free online grace period ends, turning Switch multiplayer into a subscription conversation.
items.sourceNote: Ars Technica/TechCrunch report the September 18, 2018 launch.

items.title: PS4 reaches a confident late generation
items.body: God of War and Spider-Man show Sony's first-party pipeline at full public strength.
items.sourceNote: 2018 release and awards context supports this year-level reading.

items.title: Microsoft invests in studios
items.body: The E3 2018 acquisitions signal Xbox rebuilding for a future beyond the current console cycle.
items.sourceNote: Microsoft Source confirms Playground, The Initiative and acquisition plans.

items.title: Epic Games Store appears
items.body: A new PC storefront challenges Steam with a developer-friendly revenue split and the start of an exclusivity argument.
items.sourceNote: Gematsu/TechCrunch report the December 2018 announcement/launch.

sections.title: Online Life
sections.subtitle: How the network felt around the edges of play.

items.title: Streams and servers
items.body: Online life feels like Fortnite seasons, Twitch drops, Discord servers, Reddit arguments and YouTube explainers racing ahead of print.
items.sourceNote: Online-life context is synthesised from platform history, period reporting and UK access patterns; verify with period web/magazine sources before final publication.

items.title: Services and stores
items.body: Cross-play becomes a public expectation rather than a technical curiosity, especially around Fortnite.
items.sourceNote: Online-life context is synthesised from platform history, period reporting and UK access patterns; verify with period web/magazine sources before final publication.

items.title: Forums and feeds
items.body: Nintendo Switch Online brings NES games, cloud saves and paid multiplayer into Nintendo's modern vocabulary.
items.sourceNote: Online-life context is synthesised from platform history, period reporting and UK access patterns; verify with period web/magazine sources before final publication.

items.title: Everyday connection
items.body: PC players begin arguing about launchers and storefront loyalty as Epic prepares a direct challenge to Steam.
items.sourceNote: Online-life context is synthesised from platform history, period reporting and UK access patterns; verify with period web/magazine sources before final publication.

sections.title: What It Felt Like
sections.subtitle: A curator's narrative sketch of the month as lived culture.

items.title: The room
items.body: It felt like the current generation had stopped apologising for itself. PS4, Xbox One, Switch and PC all knew what they were.
items.sourceNote: Interpretive narrative based on researched anchors; keep labelled as atmosphere rather than fact.

items.title: The shelf
items.body: The year had two kinds of scale: Red Dead's impossible detail and Celeste's tiny, devastating precision.
items.sourceNote: Interpretive narrative based on researched anchors; keep labelled as atmosphere rather than fact.

items.title: The conversation
items.body: For UK players, Forza Horizon 4 made local roads look glossy and strange, while Spider-Man and God of War made PS4 feel like the prestige box.
items.sourceNote: Interpretive narrative based on researched anchors; keep labelled as atmosphere rather than fact.

items.title: The afterimage
items.body: Underneath the big games, the future was already shifting toward subscriptions, storefronts and watching games as much as playing them.
items.sourceNote: Interpretive narrative based on researched anchors; keep labelled as atmosphere rather than fact.
